description: former skyscraper complex in Manhattan, New York (1968-2001)
description_wiki: The original World Trade Center (WTC) was a complex of seven buildings in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

aliases: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense

description: headquarters of the United States Department of Defense; named after the shape of the building, a polygon with five (greek: penta) sides
description_wiki: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense, in Arlington County, Virginia, across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. The building was constructed on an accelerated schedule during World War II. As a symbol of the U.S. military, the phrase The Pentagon is often used as a metonym for the Department of Defense and its leadership.

description: United States federal agency
description_wiki: The National Park Service (NPS) is an agency of the United States federal government, within the United States Department of the Interior.

description: one-and-a-half million acres in Florida (US) managed by the National Park Service
description_wiki: Everglades National Park  is a United States national park that protects the southern twenty percent of the original Everglades in Florida.

description: Category 5 Atlantic hurricane in 1992
description_wiki: Hurricane Andrew was the most destructive hurricane to ever hit Florida in terms of structures damaged or destroyed, and remained the costliest in financial terms until Hurricane Irma surpassed it 25 years later.

description: official residence and office of the President of the United States
description_wiki: The White House is the official residence and workplace of the President of the United States.

description: U.S. federal executive department
description_wiki: The United States Department of the Interior (DOI) is an executive department of the U.S. federal government responsible for the management and conservation of most federal lands and natural resources.

description: town in West Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Harpers Ferry is a historic town in Jefferson County, West Virginia, United States.

description_wiki: Ellis Island is an island in New York Harbor, within the U.S. states of New Jersey and New York.

aliases: The Statue of Liberty Enlightening the World
description: American icon in New York, United States
description_wiki: The Statue of Liberty (Liberty Enlightening the World; French: La Liberté éclairant le monde) is a colossal neoclassical sculpture of a robed and crowned woman on Liberty Island in New York Harbor, within New York City.

description: public park in Manhattan, New York City, New York, United States
standardized approach to command, control, and coordination of emergency response
description_wiki: The Incident Command System (ICS) is a standardized approach to the command, control, and coordination of emergency response providing a common hierarchy within which responders from multiple agencies can be effective.

description: English footballer (born 1944)
description_wiki: Dennis Burnett (born 27 September 1944) is an English former football defender.

description: first national park in the world, located in Wyoming, Montana, and Idaho in the United States
description_wiki: Yellowstone National Park is a national park of the United States located in the northwest corner of the state of Wyoming, with small portions extending into Montana and Idaho.

description: conflagration in Wyoming, Montana, Idaho, U.S.
description_wiki: The Yellowstone fires of 1988 collectively formed the largest wildfire in the recorded history of Yellowstone National Park in the United States.
